free_text: Measurements of the total neutron cross-section of
cerium and thulium in the energy range from
1.8 milli-eV to 1.8 eV
Experimental details given
Similar to journal
Atomkernenergie/Kerntechnik vol. 33, No 2, 1979, p.212
Samples were prepared from spec. pure fine oxide
powders, Ce02 and Tm203, packed in perspex containers.
Spectroscopic analysis for Ce was: Fe: 5ppm, Na: 1ppm,
Cu: < 1ppm, Mg: 1 ppm, Ag: < 1ppm,
one sample of thickness 5.11 g/cm2 was prepared.
Spectroscopic analysis for Tm was: Ca: 1ppm, Si: 2ppm,
Fe: 5ppm, Na : 1ppm and Mg: < 1ppm. One
sample of thickness 1.23 g/cm2 was prepared.
Ce02 crystallizes in a cubic system related to the
space group F(M)3(M) - 5 O (H).
Absolute measurement
Thermal reactor ET-RR-1
The spectrometer resolution at a flight path of
5.66 m and rotor speeds of 1415 rpm and 2880 rpm
is 23 microsec/m and 11 microsec/m.
No further information
No description of error analysis.
TMK,VOF
HW.-Alterations in subents 002-005,007,008.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Data digitized from figs.2,3. Subent 10 was added.
The total neutron cross section was obtained from the
total neutron cross section of CeO2 by subtraction of
the oxygen cross section incoherently.
Digitizing error
Data were digitized from fig.2 in
Atomkernenergie/Kerntechnik vol. 33, No 3, 1979, p.212.
HW.-'Deriv' added to reaction SF9, EN-MIN
altered in data section, explanation of how data has
been derived given in comment.-
SD: Data were digitized from fig.2.
'DERIV' was deleted.
Given data is in reasonable agreement with the value
(4.7 +- 0.3) b of Vertebnii, V.P.,et al.: IAEA second
Int. Conf. on Nucl. Data for Reactors, Helsinki,1970.
Data from the text of AKE,33,212,1979
HW.-Modifier 'MXW' removed from SF8 in
reaction code, 'EN' changed in common section.-
Given data was calculated using the tabulated form
factors reported by Blume,M., et al. : J. Chem. Phys.
37 (1962) 1245 and Blume,M., et al. : J. Chem. Phys.
41 (1964) 1878 and incoherently subtracted from the
obtained value of the total neutron cross-section.
Fig. of potential scattering cross-section vs. neutron
wave length and energy range of 1.8 eV to 0.02 eV is
given in Jour. of Atomkernenergie/Kerntechnik vol. 33,
No. 3. 1979, p. 212. This value was found to fluctuate
near constant value of given data.
This value is in good agreement with the value of
(2.92 +- 0.05) b of Hughes,D.J., R.Schwartz: Neutron
Cross-Sections, BNL - 325, Supplement no. 2 (1966)
and Supplement (1975)
Data from the text of AKE,33,212,1979
HW.- EN-MIN in common section altered.-
The coherent scattering amplitude was determined from
the Bragg reflections observed in the total neutron
cross section of Ce02.
Given data is in good agreement, within the statistical
accuracy, with the value of (4.8 +- 0.06) Fermi of
Hughes, D.J., R.Schwartz: Neutron Cross-Sections,
BNL - 325, Supplement no.2(1966) and Supplement (1975)
Data from the text of AKE,33,212,1979
HW.-Explanation of how data has been
derived given in comment.-
Digitizing error
Fig.3 of cross-section vs. neutron wavelength and
energy range of 1.8 milli-eV to 0.02 eV given in Jour.
Atomkernenergie/Kerntechnik vol. 33, No 3, 1979, p.212.
Data were digitized from fig.3,AKE,33,212,1979
SD: Data were digitized from fig.3.
Given data is in agreement with the value of
(134 +- 2) b of Zimmerman,R.L., et al. : Nucl. Phys.
Data from the text of AKE,33,212,1979
HW.-'MXW' and 'DERIV' removed from reaction
code, 'EN' altered in common section.-
OS. DERIV resotred in SF9
The cross section was determined by calculating the
Paramagnetic cross section and subtracting it incohe-
rently from the total cross section at all energies.
Data from the text of AKE,33,212,1979
HW.-Explanation of how data has been derive
given in comment.-
Comment by compiler: data given in abstract only.
Data taken from Jour. of
Atomkernenergie.Kerntechnik vol. 33, No.3, 1979, p.212.
OS. DERIV resotred in SF9, EN corrected
